description: Installed OWASP Juice Shop on the Metasploitable2 machine. Verified that the application was running and accessible within the target subnet.
learnings: Learned about vulnerable web applications used for practicing web security testing. Understood deployment and access within isolated environments.

description: Installed Metasploitable 2 as a target machine in the virtual lab. Configured it to be on the target subnet and ensured proper communication with other machines.
learnings: Learned about intentionally vulnerable systems used for penetration testing practice. Understood subnet configuration in a controlled lab.

description: Continued configuring Wazuh–OPNsense integration and tested log collection by generating traffic through the firewall. Verified whether logs were properly reflected in the Wazuh dashboard.
learnings: Gained understanding of how firewall logs help in identifying suspicious activities. Learned how to validate integration through log analysis.

description: Started working on integrating Wazuh with OPNsense to enable centralized log monitoring from the firewall. Explored log forwarding methods and configured initial settings to allow OPNsense logs to be sent to the Wazuh manager.
learnings: Learned how SIEM tools collect logs from network devices like firewalls. Understood the importance of log forwarding for real-time monitoring and threat detection.

description: Worked on creating seminar content with proper examples and diagrams. Focused on making explanations clear and engaging.
learnings: Enhanced ability to communicate complex technical concepts. Learned effective content structuring.

description: Prepared for internship seminar by selecting relevant cybersecurity topics. Planned demonstrations and structured explanations.
learnings: Improved conceptual clarity and presentation planning. Learned how to simplify technical topics.

description: Reviewed the overall lab performance and verified log consistency across Wazuh and Zeek. Prepared technical notes for the upcoming seminar preparation.
learnings: Understood the importance of cross-tool validation in ensuring data accuracy within a security operations environment.

description: Performed additional testing to ensure stability of configurations. Verified network communication and monitoring outputs.
learnings: Learned the importance of repeated testing in maintaining system reliability. Strengthened troubleshooting abilities.

description: Reviewed the complete cybersecurity lab including Wazuh, Zeek, and Caldera. Verified that all tools were functioning correctly together.
learnings: Developed a holistic understanding of integrated cybersecurity systems. Learned how multiple tools complement each other.

description: Documented all observations from simulations and monitoring tools. Organized results for reporting and future reference.
learnings: Improved documentation skills and structured reporting. Learned importance of clear record keeping.

description: Correlated attack simulations with alerts generated in Wazuh. Observed detection and logging of malicious activities.
learnings: Understood integration of red teaming and blue teaming tools. Learned how detection systems respond to simulated attacks.

description: Executed basic adversary simulations using Caldera. Observed system responses and monitored behavior during simulated attacks.
learnings: Learned how real-world attack techniques are simulated. Understood attacker behavior patterns.

description:
What I worked on?
Continued configuring Caldera and explored its features including agents and adversary profiles. Prepared the environment for simulation.
learnings: Understood how attack scenarios are structured and executed. Learned about automated adversary behavior.

description: Researched different adversary emulation profiles in MITRE Caldera and studied how they map to the ATT&CK framework.
learnings: Gained a deeper understanding of how structured attack patterns help in improving detection capabilities.

description: Started installation of MITRE Caldera for adversary simulation. Understood its purpose in red teaming.
learnings: Learned basics of adversary emulation and attack simulation.

description: Verified if Wazuh setup was functioning correctly. Checked logs, alerts, and agent status.
learnings: Learned system validation and monitoring techniques.

description: Tested connectivity using ping and other tools. Ensured attacker and target machines were properly communicating.
learnings: Strengthened understanding of network communication validation.

description: Configured Kali Linux network settings. Attempted to change IP address to ensure proper communication with other machines.

description: Worked on improving virtual lab setup by modifying network adapters. Experimented with NAT and host-only networks for better communication.

description: Explored advanced features of Wazuh including rule sets and alert severity levels. Analyzed different types of alerts.
learnings: Understood how SIEM tools classify and prioritize threats.

description: Explored advanced SIEM concepts including log normalization and correlation rules. Studied how Wazuh handles different log formats from various sources.
learnings: Learned about the backend processing of logs and how normalized data helps in efficient threat hunting.

description: Tested Wazuh by generating logs and monitoring alerts on dashboard. Verified that agent logs were successfully reaching manager.
learnings: Learned real-time log monitoring and alert generation.

description: Troubleshot connectivity issues between machines. Worked on fixing IP configuration and ensuring systems could ping each other successfully.
learnings: Improved understanding of network troubleshooting and VM communication.

description: Worked on installing Wazuh agent on the target machine. Connected agent to manager by configuring IP and authentication keys.
learnings: Learned how endpoint monitoring works through agents.

description: Installed Wazuh manager on the management machine. Configured basic settings and ensured services were running properly. Verified installation through dashboard access.
learnings: Understood backend components of Wazuh and how logs are processed centrally.

description: Started exploring SIEM tools and began setup of Wazuh. Understood its architecture including manager, agent, and dashboard components. Prepared virtual machines for deployment.
blockers: Initial confusion in architecture setup.
learnings: Learned the role of SIEM in cybersecurity and how Wazuh helps in threat detection and log analysis.

description: Summarized the entire learning experience and reviewed key concepts including firewall configuration, IDS challenges, and network monitoring tools.
learnings: Developed a strong foundation in practical cybersecurity lab setup and analysis.

description: Summarized the findings from Zeek log analysis and practiced identifying common network attack patterns in the logs.
learnings: Improved ability to distinguish between normal traffic and potentially malicious network activity.

description: Reviewed complete setup including OPNsense and Zeek integration. Verified firewall rules and monitoring outputs.

description: Documented all configurations and findings from firewall and monitoring tools. Organized observations for better understanding and future reference.

description: Generated network traffic using attacker machine to test monitoring capabilities. Observed how Zeek logs different activities such as DNS queries and connections.
learnings: Learned practical testing of monitoring tools in simulated attack scenarios.

description: Studied concepts of IDS, IPS, and network monitoring. Compared Suricata and Zeek in terms of functionality and use cases.

description: Analyzed suspicious traffic patterns using Zeek logs. Identified anomalies and studied network behavior under different conditions.

description: Integrated Zeek with firewall setup. Observed real-time traffic and analyzed logs for different types of network activities.

description: Configured Zeek in the lab environment. Monitored network traffic and explored generated logs such as connection, DNS, and HTTP logs.

blockers: Persistent configuration issues.

blockers: Alerts were not triggering as expected.

description: Reviewed firewall configuration best practices and practiced designing complex rule sets for multi-tier network architectures.
learnings: Strengthened understanding of the 'least privilege' principle in network security and firewall management.

description: Configured firewall rules in OPNsense to control traffic flow. Created rules to allow and block specific traffic and tested them using different network scenarios. Observed how packets are filtered based on rules.
learnings: Learned rule-based packet filtering and importance of firewall policies in securing networks.

description: Installed and configured OPNsense firewall in a virtual machine. Assigned WAN and LAN interfaces, configured IP addressing, and accessed the web interface for further setup. Verified connectivity between internal and external networks.
blockers: Initial confusion in interface mapping, later resolved.
learnings: Understood firewall architecture, interface assignment, and basic routing concept

description: Set up the cybersecurity virtual lab environment by installing and configuring virtual machines. Ensured proper allocation of system resources for smooth performance.

description: Reviewed machine setup, CTF challenges, and lab configurations. Ensured all systems were functioning correctly and documented findings.

blockers: Subnet calculations required repeated practice for accuracy.
learnings: Learned how subnetting improves network security through segmentation. Understood asymmetric encryption, key pairs, and how they are used in secure protocols like HTTPS and SSH.

blockers: Complexity in understanding resource management and networking modes initially

description: Installation and configuration of the Stapler vulnerable VM. Troubleshot boot and driver issues. Configured networking settings in virtualization software. Learned how firewalls monitor and filter incoming and outgoing traffic based on rules. Understood packet filtering, stateful inspection, and role of firewalls in preventing unauthorized acces

blockers: Some levels required combining multiple commands effectively.
learnings: Improved Linux proficiency and understood how misconfigured permissions can lead to security vulnerabilities.

description: Explored advanced file signature analysis and practiced identifying unknown file types using hex editors and magic bytes.
learnings: Gained practical experience in low-level file analysis and its application in forensics.

blockers: Initial difficulty identifying correct byte sequences.
learnings: Understood how file signatures help in digital forensics and how attackers can disguise malicious files by altering headers.

description: ntegrated permission checks into the Shell project and studied process management (fork, exec concepts). Explored how privilege levels affect command execution.
blockers: Understanding process creation workflow required additional practice.
learnings: Learned about privilege escalation risks and how access control mechanisms prevent unauthorized operations.

description: Continued development of the Shell project. Focused on command parsing, built-in commands, execution flow, and improving error handling. Tested edge cases and invalid input scenarios.
blockers: Debugging command parsing logic required multiple iterations.
learnings: Understood how shells interact with the operating system using system calls. Learned importance of input validation and secure execution practices.

description: Final refinement of Shell and File System projects. Cleaned code, improved documentation, and reviewed security mechanisms implemented.
learnings: Strengthened understanding of secure coding and documentation practices.

blockers: UI alignment and command mapping issues.
learnings: Learned how shells enforce security boundaries to prevent misuse. Understood the balance between usability and security in system tools

blockers: Command parsing complexity. Permission-related execution issues.
learnings: Learned how shells interpret commands and how improper handling can lead to security vulnerabilities. Improved understanding of OS-level command execution and error management.

description: Self-study on file system security and access control lists (ACLs). Researched how different operating systems enforce file-level permissions.
learnings: Understood the fundamental differences between Windows and Linux permission models.

description: Worked on designing and developing a Virtual File System using Python with support for file and directory operations, virtual storage persistence, and user interaction through both GUI and terminal interfaces.
learnings: Gained practical understanding of file system concepts by implementing a Python-based Virtual File System, including inode structures, directory management, and file operations, along with experience in GUI and TUI development

description: Implemented core features of the Virtual File System including file creation, deletion, and directory navigation. Focused on maintaining isolation and preventing unauthorized access.
learnings: Understood how process isolation and permission checks enhance system security.

description: Studied operating system security with focus on Windows and Linux security concepts including user permissions and access control.
learnings: Learned how OS-level security helps prevent unauthorized access and system compromise.

description: Introduction to networking basics relevant to cybersecurity such as IP addressing, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, and network attacks.

learnings: Understood how process isolation and permission checks enhance system security.
